title: Understanding the Effect of Accuracy on Trust in Machine Learning Models
calibration_notes: No calibration accuracy measurement. Undertrust/distrust discussed
key_finding:
6 DISCUSSION
In this paper, we investigate whether the accuracy of an ML
model affects laypeople’s willingness to trust the model via a
sequence of large-scale, randomized, pre-registered human-
subject experiments. We find that a model’s stated accuracy
on held-out data affects people’s trust in the model, but that
the effect size is smaller after people observe the model’s
accuracy in practice. Furthermore, if a model’s observed ac-
curacy is low, then its stated accuracy has at most a very...

in AI OR Under/Over-Confdent in Oneself? Previous studies on AI-assisted decision-making have often focused 
on calibrating people’s trust in AI when promoting appropriate 
reliance [2, 89, 90]. We propose that inappropriate reliance can arise from both inap-
propriate trust in AI and inappropriate confdence in oneself.

using 
anthropomorphism to conceptualize trust in robots. Discussion
With the growing adoption of AI chatbots in education, it is impor -
tant to understand how trust in these systems should be measured and 
modeled. The extent to which students place trust in AI-enabled 
educational technologies has influence on whether students engage in 
productive behaviors such as critical thinking and sustained interaction, 
or fall into disengagement and over-reliance that undermine learning.
